score.finalText:
Westfield Collective is in **closeout, but it is not clean closeout**. The biggest issue right now is a **possible warranty/spec miss on a door in a controlled environment**, and there’s also **collections friction** hanging over the job.

### Hard facts

- **Most recent signal — today, May 12:** Mike Ewers emailed Andrew Cannon saying outside air was coming through the entire door, not just the perimeter, and that the installed door is **not rated for controlled environments**. He referenced the installed door as **R2** and said controlled spaces should be **no less than R8**.  
  Source: email dated **2026-05-12**.

- **Commercial/payment pressure — May 11:** Brandon followed up on **two invoices over 60 days late** and asked for payment status.  
  Source: email dated **2026-05-11**.

- **Open decision on repair scope/pricing — May 7:** Andrew asked Gordon Chapman for **two separate patio repair prices** before making a decision. That tells me the repair path was **not yet finalized** at that point.  
  Source: email dated **2026-05-07**.

- **Invoice/pay app ambiguity — May 7:** Andrew asked whether the billing period for **App 7** was in January. That suggests there was still some billing clarification happening during closeout.  
  Source: email dated **2026-05-07**.

- **Small change/commercial loose end — Apr 29:** Grey Rock said they **didn’t do the joint fill**, had already submitted the pay app, and suggested **$4,000** unless pay apps were redone.  
  Source: email dated **2026-04-29**.

- **Last solid meeting trail is old:** The meeting history I found is mostly **Jan–Feb 2026 OACs**, when the team was driving toward TCO/turnover and finish work. I’m not seeing a fresh meeting trail that shows all closeout items are fully burned down.

### What changed

The

results.summary:
✅ Finish Work Completion: Casework and door installations in commissary hall to finish this week, with tile work nearly done.
🛑 Signage Approval: Permits approved for Westfield Collective signs; installation planned this weekend if weather permits.
📅 TCO Target Date: Final turnover certificate (TCO) expected week of February 23 due to kitchen equipment delays.
📦 Equipment Deliveries: Micromatic equipment shipment on track for arrival between Friday and Monday, with installation next week.
🛠️ Safety Measures: Mezzanine railing safety cap design pending approval, aiming to prevent falls and contain objects.
📢 Communication Update: Andrew Cannon to share meeting notes and change order pricing by week’s end to keep everyone informed.

Wrap up casework installations and roof rails verification (02:12)

Ensure suitable treatment of gaps under banquettes using caulk rather than vinyl base wrap to maintain aesthetics (10:20)
Plan delivery of Micromatic equipment for the week following shipment and schedule installation crew accordingly, aiming for Thursday completion (12:58)
Evaluate possibility of starting first floor work earlier and communicate availability of crew (14:13)

results.title: OAC- Westfield Collective
results.summary: Construction progress is on track with FRP installation at 95% and key tasks such as beer wall tiling and kitchen equipment installation scheduled for next week. The project timeline includes a final punch walk on February 5 and aims for final inspections in March, aligning cleaning efforts with these milestones. Electrical compliance remains critical, with lights expected by the end of January and fire alarm setups needing completion by January 31 to meet inspection deadlines. Design changes include reduced seating for better flow and commissioned murals for $7,500-$8,000, targeted for completion by early March. Vendor coordination is essential, with Pepsi installation beginning next week and signage installation planned to enhance brand visibility. Continuous communication and collaborat

results.title: OAC- Westfield Collective
results.summary: Construction is advancing into the finishing phase, with key installations like Lemur wallpaper and tile work currently underway. The installation of wall-mounted toilets and FRP panels in the kitchen commissary is planned, alongside significant electrical work, including outlet installations. Critical equipment deliveries, particularly for draft beer systems, depend on timely payment processing, with upcoming inspections and a Temporary Certificate of Occupancy targeted by February 11. Design adjustments aim to balance aesthetics and functionality, such as using white FRP panels to reduce costs. The team is also managing excess materials for future job sites to minimize waste and ensure budget control.

results.title: Westfield Collective: ERP Issues
results.summary: The team discussed several critical issues affecting invoice exports and financial accuracy. Key problems identified included failures in invoice exports largely due to unsynced change orders in the ERP system, particularly affecting vendors like Superior and projects such as Westfield Collective. Andrew Cannon highlighted that some change orders remained in draft status, blocking exports, while Accounting recommended pushing change items to ERP and approving them. Duplicate vendor contacts also complicated invoice processing, leading to proposed merging strategies. Furthermore, it was noted that once approved, invoice contacts cannot be changed, causing processing delays. Escalation of unresolved sync errors to Kristen was deemed necessary, and specific deductive change orders were also c

results.title: Westfield Collective Design Review Meeting
results.summary:
**Executive Summary of the Westfield Collective Design Review Meeting**

**Meeting Purpose and Key Participants**  
The Westfield Collective Design Review Meeting was convened on October 15, 2023, to evaluate and finalize design proposals for the upcoming Westfield Collective project. Key participants included Sarah Thompson, the Project Manager; David Lee, the Lead Architect; and Emily Chen, the Marketing Director. The meeting aimed to ensure that all design elements align with the project's vision and stakeholder expectations while addressing any potential challenges.
